zh.lifeSummary: 當William Gregg於1659年10月17日出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安卡林頓時，他的父親John Greag33歲，母親Jeane Lawsoune34歲。他於1670年8月4日與Jean Brown在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪聖 尼古拉斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Gregg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): from the Middle English personal name Greg, a pet form of Gregory ; compare Grigg .

variants.name: Cregg
meanings: English (Lancashire) and Irish: perhaps a variant of Gregg with devoicing of the initial g.

variants.name: Greggs
meanings: English: variant of Gregg , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Gregson
meanings: English (Lancashire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Greg (see Gregg ), a short form of the personal name Gregory + son.

variants.name: Gregory
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from a personal name that was popular throughout Christendom in the Middle Ages. The Greek original, Grēgorios, is a derivative of grēgorein ‘to be awake, to be watchful’. However, the Latin form, Gregorius, came to be associated by folk etymology with grex, gregis ‘flock, herd’, under the influence of the Christian image of the good shepherd. The Greek name was borne in the early Christian centuries by two fathers of the Orthodox Church, Saint Gregory Nazianzene (c. 325–390) and Saint Gregory of Nyssa (c. 331–395), and later by sixteen popes, starting with Gregory the Great (c. 540–604). It was also the name of 3rd- and 4th-century apostles of Armenia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Gregorio , German, Slovak, and Slovenian Gregor , Polish Grzegorz, Czech Řehoř (see Rehor ), and French Gregoire , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Polish Grzegorczyk .

variants.name: Gragg
meanings: English: variant of   Gregg   and possibly also of   Cragg  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Gregg was born on 17 October 1659, in Carrington, Midlothian, Scotland, his father, John Greag, was 33 and his mother, Jeane Lawsoune, was 34. He married Jean Brown on 4 August 1670, in St Nicholas, Aberdeen, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.

ko.lifeSummary: William Gregg(이)가 1659년 10월 17일 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 카링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Greag(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeane Lawsoune(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1670년 8월 4일 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디 스트 닉홀아스에서 Jean Brown(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.
